channel.title: Conversational Old Testament on Christian Internet Resources
channel.description: Recent content in Conversational Old Testament on Christian Internet Resources

item.title: 1 Chronicles 1
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 1

1 Adam father of Seth father of Enosh father of 2 Kenan father of Mahalalel father of Jared father of 3 Enoch father of Methuselah father of Lamech father of 4 Noah father of Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

5 The sons of Japheth: Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, Tiras

 6The sons of Gomer were Ashkenaz, Diphath, Togarmah;

item.title: 1 Kings 1
item.description:
1 Kings Chapter 1

David in His Old Age

1 When King David got old, they covered him with blankets, but he still had the chills. 2 His advisers told him, “Let us look for a young virgin so she can attend you, become your nurse, and lie in your arms to keep you warm.” 3 They searched throughout Israel for a beautiful girl and found Abishag the Shunammite, and brought her to the king. 4 She became his nurse, but he didn’t have relations with her.

item.title: 1 Samuel 1
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 1

The Birth of Samuel

1 Elkanah was from Ramathaim-zophim in the hill country of Ephraim. He was the son of Jeroham son of Elihu (son of Tohu son of Zuph), an Ephraimite. 2 He had two wives: Hannah and Peninnah. Peninnah had children but Hannah didn’t.

item.title: 2 Chronicles 1
item.description:
2 Chronicles Chapter 1

Solomon Asks for Wisdom

1 Solomon secured himself in his kingdom; the LORD God was with him and made him great. 2 He called together all Israel--the commanders of thousands and hundreds, the judges and every leader, the heads of families. 3 He led the whole assembly went to the high place at Gibeon; God’s Tent of Meeting was there that Moses had made in the countryside. 4 But David had brought the Ark up from Kiriath-jearim to the tent he’d made for it in Jerusalem. 5 The bronze altar that Bezalel son of Uri son of Hur had made, was there in front of the Tabernacle. Solomon and the assembly went to it, 6 and he offered 1,000 burnt offerings on it.                                                       2 Ch 1:1-6

item.title: 2 Kings 1
item.description:
2 Kings Chapter 1

The LORD’s Judgment on Ahaziah

1 After Ahab died, Moab revolted against Israel.  

2 Ahaziah fell through the lattice in his upper room in Samaria and seriously injured himself. He told messengers to go ask Baal-zebub, Ekron’s god, if he’d recover. 3 But the angel of the LORD told Elijah the Tishbite,

item.title: 2 Samuel 1
item.description:
2 Samuel Chapter 1

David Learns of Saul’s Death

1 After Saul died, David came back from massacring the Amalekites and stayed two days in Ziklag. 2 The third day, a man came from Saul’s camp with torn clothes and dust on his head. He prostrated himself to David.

3 David said, “Where have you come from?”

“I’ve escaped from Israel’s camp.”

item.title: 1 Chronicles 2
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 2

1 The sons of Israel: Reuben, Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, Zebulun, 2 Dan, Joseph, Benjamin,

            Naphtali, Gad, and Asher

3 The sons of JUDAH: Er, Onan, and Shelah by Bath-shua the Canaanitess

            Er, Judah’s firstborn, was sinful; so the LORD took his life.

item.title: 1 Kings 2
item.description:
1 Kings Chapter 2

David’s Instructions to Solomon

1 As David’s time to die came closer, he charged Solomon,

2 “I’m going the way of all the earth. So be strong, show yourself to be a man. 3 Keep the charge of the LORD your God, follow him, keep his statutes, commandments, ordinances, and testimonies written in the Law of Moses. Then you can succeed at everything you do. 4 The LORD will carry out his promise to me, ‘If your descendants follow me sincerely with their heart and soul, you’ll always have one on Israel’s throne.’ 5 You also know what Joab did to me and the two commanders of Israel’s armies, Abner and Amasa that he murdered. He shed the blood of war in the time of peace. He put the blood of war on the belt around his waist and the sandals on his feet. 6 Act wisely; don’t let his gray hair go down to Sheol in peace. 7 But be kind to the descendants of Barzillai the Gileadite. Have them eat at your table. They helped me when I was eluding your half-brother Absalom. 8 Shimei son of Gera is with you. He cursed me with violent curses when I went to Mahanaim. But when he came down to me at the Jordan, I swore to him by the LORD that I wouldn’t put him to death with the sword. 9 Don’t you let him go unpunished. You’re a wise man; send his gray hair down to Sheol with blood.”

item.title: 1 Samuel 2
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 2

He worshipped the LORD there.

1 Sam 1:21-28

Hannah’s Prayer

1 Then Hannah prayed,

                        “My heart celebrates in the LORD;

                              you’ve honored me.

                        I speak boldly against my adversaries

                              because I celebrate over your salvation.

                        2 There’s no one holy like the LORD;

item.title: 2 Chronicles 2
item.description:
2 Chronicles Chapter 2

Preparations for Building the Temple

1 Solomon decided to build a temple for the LORD’s name and a royal palace for himself. 2 He assigned 70,000 to transport materials, 80,000 to quarry stone in the mountains, and 3,600 to supervise them. 3 He sent word to Hiram, king of Tyre,

“As you sent my father David cedars for his palace, do for me. 4 I’m getting ready to build a temple for the LORD my God and dedicate it to him for burning fragrant incense and offering burnt offerings morning and evening on Sabbaths, new moons, and the LORD’s appointed festivals—items of worship required permanently in Israel. 5 The temple I’m going to build will be great, because our God is greater than all other gods. 6 But who can build him a temple? The sky, the highest heavens, can’t contain him; who am I to build him a temple, except for burning incense to him? 7 Send me a man skilled at working in gold, silver, brass, and iron; in purple, crimson, and violet fabrics; a man that knows how to engrave and work with the skilled men I have in Judah and Jerusalem that my father provided. 8 Send me cedar, cypress, and algum wood from Lebanon; your woodsmen know how to cut timber in Lebanon. My men will work with yours 9 to prepare an abundance of it for the great, wonderful temple I’m going to build. 10 I’ll give your woodsmen that cut the logs 220,000 bushels 20,000 kors of crushed wheat, 220,000 bushels 20,000 kors of barley, 189,000 gallons 20,000 baths of wine, and 189,000 gallons 20,000 baths of oil.” 2 Ch 2:1-10

item.title: 2 Kings 2
item.description:
2 Kings Chapter 2

Elijah Taken Up to Heaven

1 The LORD was getting ready to take Elijah up in a tornado. Elijah went with Elisha from Gilgal 2 and told him, “Stay here. The LORD has sent me to Bethel.”

“As sure as the LORD lives and you live, I won’t leave you.” They went down to Bethel. 3 The company of prophets in Bethel came out to Elisha, “Do you know the LORD’s going to take your master away today?”

item.title: 2 Samuel 2
item.description:
2 Samuel Chapter 2

David Anointed King of Judah

1 Afterwards David asked the LORD, “Should I go up to one of the towns in Judah?”

“Go on up.”

“Where?”

“To Hebron.”

David and his two wives went there. 3 He brought his men up with their families, and they lived in towns around Hebron. 4 Then the people in Judah came and anointed him king over Judah.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 3
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 3

         3:1These were David’s six sons born in Hebron:

                        the firstborn was Ammon by Ahinoam the Jezreelitess;

                        the second was Daniel by Abigail the Carmelitess;

                        2 the third was Absalom son of Maacah daughter of Talmai, king of Geshur;

item.title: 1 Kings 3
item.description:
1 Kings Chapter 3

Solomon Asks for Wisdom

1 Solomon made a marriage alliance with Pharaoh and brought his daughter to The City of David till he’d finished building his own palace, the LORD’s Temple, and the wall around Jerusalem. 2 People were still sacrificing on the high places; the LORD didn’t have a temple yet.

item.title: 1 Samuel 3
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 3

The Call of Samuel

1 The boy Samuel continued to serve the LORD with Eli. A message from the LORD rarely came at that time; visions weren’t frequent. 2 Eli was lying down. (His eyesight had gotten bad.) 3 The LORD’s lamp hadn’t gone out yet, and Samuel was lying down in the Tabernacle where the Ark of God was. 4 The LORD called out to him. He said, “Yes?” 5 He ran to Eli and said, “You called.” But he said, “I didn’t call; lie back down.” So he went and lay down. 6 The LORD called out again, “Samuel.” He got up and went to Eli, “You called.” But he answered, “I didn’t call you, son; lie back down.” 7 Samuel didn’t know the LORD yet, and the LORD hadn’t revealed a message to him before. 8 The LORD called out to him the third time. He got up and went to Eli, “You called.” Eli surmised that the LORD was calling the boy. 9 He told him, “Go lie down. If he calls you, say, ‘Yes, LORD; I’m listening.’” So he went and lay down.

item.title: 2 Chronicles 3
item.description:
2 Chronicles Chapter 3

Solomon Builds the Temple

1 Solomon started building the LORD’s Temple on Mount Moriah, where the Lord had appeared to David, the place David prepared on the threshing floor of Ornan/Araunah the Jebusite. 2 He began construction on the second day, second month, fourth year of his reign.

3 The foundation for God’s Temple was 30 x 90ft.

item.title: 2 Kings 3
item.description:
2 Kings Chapter 3

Moab Revolts

1 JehoramII son of Ahab became king of Israel in Samaria in the eighteenth year of Jehoshaphat king of Judah, and ruled for 12 years. 2 He did evil, though not as bad as his parents. He did put away Baal’s sacred pillar his father had built, 3 but he held onto the sins of JeroboamI that got Israel to sin.

item.title: 2 Samuel 3
item.description:
2 Samuel Chapter 3

1 A long war had gone on between Saul and David. David had grown stronger; Saul had grown weaker.

2 David had six sons in Hebron: Amnon his oldest by Ahinoam the Jezreelitess, 3 Chileab second by Abigail, the widow of Nabal of Carmel; Absalom third by Maacah daughter of Tahmai, king of Geshur; 4 Adonijah fourth by Haggith, Shephatiah fifth by Abital, 5 and Ithream sixth by Eglah.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 4
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 4

1 The sons of JUDAH: Perez, Hezron, Carmi, Hur, and Shobal

         2 Reaiah son of Shobal was father of Jahath father of Ahumai and Lahad. These were the families of the Zorathites.

         3 These were the sons of Etam: Jezreel, Ishma, and Idbash; their sister was Hazzelelponi.

         4 Penuel was father of Gedor and Ezer father of Hushah. Those were the sons of Hur, firstborn of Ephrathah ancestor of Bethlehem.

item.title: 1 Kings 4
item.description:
1 Kings Chapter 4

Solomon’s Officials and Governors

1 Solomon ruled all Israel. 2 These were his officials:

Azariah son of Zadok was priest.

                  3Elihoreph and Ahijah, sons of Shisha, were secretaries.

Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was recorder.

                  4Benaiah son of Jehoiada was the general over the army.

Zadok and Abiathar were priests.

                  5Azariah son of Nathan oversaw the deputies.

item.title: 1 Samuel 4
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 4

The Philistines Capture the Ark

1 Samuel’s word went out to all Israel.

[4:1]Israel had gone out to meet the Philistines in battle and camped beside Ebenezer. The Philistines camped in Aphek 2 and drew up in formation against Israel. When the battle spread, the Philistines defeated Israel and killed about 4,000. 3 When the troops came to camp, Israel’s elders said,

item.title: 2 Chronicles 4
item.description:
2 Chronicles Chapter 4

Temple Furnishings

1 He made

                  a bronze altar 30 x 30 x 15ft high; 

         2a circular, cast-metal sea 15ft from brim to brim, 7½ft deep and 45ft around.  

         3Figures of gourds were under it, entirely circling the sea (45ft). The oxen were in 2 rows cast in one piece with the Sea. 4 It stood on 12 oxen, 3 facing out in each direction with their hindquarters inward. 5 It was 4ins thick, with a brim like a cup—like a lily blossom. It could hold 27,000 gallons 3,000 baths.

item.title: 2 Kings 4
item.description:
2 Kings Chapter 4

The Widow’s Oil Jar

1 The wife of a man from the company of prophets cried out to Elisha, “My husband’s dead. You know how he respected the LORD. A creditor has come to take my two children as his slaves.”

2 “What can I do? What do you have in the house?”

“Nothing but a jug of oil.”

item.title: 2 Samuel 4
item.description:
2 Samuel Chapter 4

Ish-Bosheth Murdered

1 When Ish-bosheth heard that Abner had died in Hebron, his courage failed, and Israel lost courage too. 2 Saul’s son had two men that were commanding raiding bands: Baanah and Rechab, sons of Rimmon, a Benjamite from Beeroth (considered part of Benjamin). 3 The people of Beeroth fled to Gittaim and still live there as foreigners.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 5
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 5

5:1, 3The sons of REUBEN, Israel’s firstborn: 3Hanoch and Pallu, Hezron and Carmi

 (Because he defiled his father’s bed, his birthright was given to the sons of Joseph son of Israel; so he’s not enrolled in the genealogy by birthright. 2 Although Judah prevailed over his brothers and the leader came from him, the birthright was Joseph’s.)

item.title: 1 Kings 5
item.description:
1 Kings Chapter 5

Preparations for Building the Temple

1 Hiram, king of Tyre, sent ambassadors to Solomon when he heard they’d anointed him king in place of his father. He’d always been a friend to David. 2 Solomon sent him a message,

3 “You know my father couldn’t build a temple for the LORD his God because he carried on warfare till the LORD put down his enemies. 4 But now the LORD has given me peace in every direction. There’s no adversary or misfortune. 5 I intend to build a temple for the LORD like he said, ‘Your son that I’ll set on your throne will build me a temple.’ 6 Have your woodsmen cut cedars from Lebanon, and my workmen will work with yours. I’ll pay them whatever wages you set. You know there’s nobody among us that knows how to cut timber like Sidonians.”

item.title: 1 Samuel 5
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 5

The Philistines and the Ark

1 The Philistines carried the Ark from Ebenezer to Ashdod 2 to Dagon’s temple and set it up beside him. 3 When people got up early the next morning, Dagon had fallen face down on the ground in front of the Ark of the LORD. They put Dagon back in place. 4 But when they got up early the next day, he’d fallen face down on the ground in front of the Ark of the LORD, and his head and hands were lying cut off on the threshold. Only his trunk was left. 5 That’s why Dagon’s priests and everybody that goes in his temple still don’t step on his threshold in Ashdod.

item.title: 2 Chronicles 5
item.description:
2 Chronicles Chapter 5

1 When Solomon finished the work on the LORD’s Temple, he brought in the silver, gold, and utensils David had dedicated, and put them in the treasuries in God’s Temple.

The Ark Brought to the Temple

2 Then he gathered to Jerusalem the elders in Israel, the leaders of tribes and families, in preparation for bringing up the Ark from The City of David (Zion). 3 The men of Israel gathered to him at the festival in the seventh month. 4 The elders came, and the Levites picked up the Ark 5 and brought it up. They brought up the Tent of Meeting and the holy utensils that were in it. 6 King Solomon and the community were sacrificing so many sheep and oxen they couldn’t keep count of them. 7 The priests brought the Ark to its place in the inner sanctuary, the Holy of Holies, under the cherubs. 8 They spread their wings over it and covered it and its poles. 9 The poles were long enough to be visible in front of the inner sanctuary, but not outside; they’re still there. 10 The only things in the Ark were the two tablets Moses put there at Horeb/Sinai, where the LORD made a covenant with the Israelites after they came out of Egypt.                                                               2 Ch 5:1-10

item.title: 2 Kings 5
item.description:
2 Kings Chapter 5

Naaman’s Leprosy Healed

1 Naaman, commander of Aram’s army, was a great man with his master and highly respected, because by him the LORD had given victory to Aram. He was a mighty soldier, but he was a leper. 2 The Arameans had gone out in bands and taken captive a little girl from Israel, who became a maid for Naaman’s wife. 3 She told her mistress, “I wish my master would go see the prophet that’s in Samaria. He’d cure his leprosy.”

item.title: 2 Samuel 5
item.description:
2 Samuel Chapter 5

David Becomes King of Israel

            1 All the tribes came to David in Hebron and said,

“We’re your flesh and bones. 2 When Saul was king, you were the one that led Israel out and back. The LORD told you you’d shepherd his people.”

 3 Israel’s elders came, and King David made a treaty with them in the LORD’s presence. They anointed him king over Israel. 4 David was 30 and ruled for 40 years. 5 He ruled Judah for 7½ years in Hebron and ruled Judah and Israel for 33 years in Jerusalem.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 6
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 6

1 The descendants of LEVI: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari

         2 The descendants of Kohath were Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.

                  3 The children of Amram were Aaron, Moses, and Miriam.

                           The sons of Aaron were Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.

item.title: 1 Kings 6
item.description:
1 Kings Chapter 6

Solomon Builds the Temple

1 In the 480th year after the Israelites left Egypt, in the fourth year of Solomon’s reign, the second month (Ziv), he started building the LORD’s Temple. 2 It was 90 x 30 x 45ft high. 3 The vestibule across the nave was 30ft long—equal to the Temple’s width—and 15ft deep. 4 He made recessed frames for the windows. 5 He also built a three-story structure against the Temple walls, going around the nave and inner sanctuary, and made side rooms around the outside. 6 The first story was 7½ft wide, the middle one 9ft, and the third 10½ft; because on the outside he made offsets in the wall of the Temple all the way around so the beams didn’t insert into the Temple walls. 7 While the Temple was being built, the stones were prepared at the quarry. There wasn’t any sound of hammer, axe, or iron tool on site. 8 The entry for the lowest side room was on the right side of the Temple. People could go up winding stairs to the middle and third stories. 9 He built the Temple and finished it with beams and cedar planks 10 in addition to the three stories, each 7½ft high, resting against the Temple wall on cedar timbers.          1 Kg 6:1-10

item.title: 1 Samuel 6
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 6

The Ark Returns to Israel

1 The LORD’s Ark was in Philistine territory for seven months. 2 They called for the priests and fortunetellers, “What should we do with it? Tell us how to send it back.”

3 “Send it back with a guilt offering to Israel’s God. You’ll be healed, and you’ll find out why he keeps working against you.”

item.title: 2 Chronicles 6
item.description:
2 Chronicles Chapter 6

Solomon Praises the LORD

1 Then Solomon said,

“You’ve said you’d be in the dense cloud.

2 I’ve built you a lofty temple,

a permanent place for you to live.”

3 Then he faced the people and blessed the assembly standing there,

4 “Bless the LORD God of Israel, who said to my father David, 5 ‘From the time I brought my people out of Egypt, I hadn’t chosen a city to build a temple in for my name. I hadn’t chosen any man as a leader over Israel. 6 But I’ve chosen Jerusalem as the place to identify with and David as leader over Israel. 7 My father wanted to build him a temple, 8 but the LORD said, ‘You meant well, 9 but you won’t build me a temple; your son will.’ 10 He has fulfilled that promise; I’ve risen to the throne and built the temple for his name. 11 That’s where I’ve set the Ark that holds the covenant he made with Israel.”

item.title: 2 Kings 6
item.description:
2 Kings Chapter 6

The Floating Ax head

1 The school of the prophets told Elisha,

“There’s not enough room where we’re living. 2 Let us go to the Jordan, and each take a log to build a place there to live.”

“Go ahead.”

3 One of them asked him to come along                               

            4 He went along to the Jordan, and they started cutting down trees.

item.title: 2 Samuel 6
item.description:
2 Samuel Chapter 6

The Ark Brought to Jerusalem

1 David gathered elite troops from Israel again, 30,000. 2 He and the troops went to Baale-judah to bring up God’s Ark called The Name: the LORD of Hosts Enthroned Above the Cherubs. 3 They placed the Ark on a new cart to bring it from Abinadab’s house that was on the hill. Uzzah and Ahio, Abinadab’s sons, 4 were bringing the Ark on the cart; Ahio was walking ahead of it. 5 Meanwhile, David and the House of Israel were celebrating enthusiastically in the LORD’s presence with all kinds of instruments made out of fir: lyres, harps, tambourines, castanets, and cymbals.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 7
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 7

1 ISSACHAR had four sons: Tola, Puah, Jashub, and Shimron.

            2 The sons of Tola were Uzzi, Rephaiah, Jeriel, Jahmai, Ibsam, and Samuel, heads of

            families. Tola’s sons were trained soldiers in their generations. At the time

            of David they numbered 22,600.

                  3 The son of Uzzi was Izrahiah.

item.title: 1 Kings 7
item.description:
1 Kings Chapter 7

Solomon Builds His Palace

1 It took 13 years to build Solomon’s palace.

2 He built the Palace of the Forest of Lebanon: 150 x 75 x 45ft high. It rested on 4 rows of cedar pillars with cedar beams on the pillars. 3 It had cedar panels above the side-rooms that were on the 45 pillars, 15 to a row. 4 There were 3 rows of window frames with window across from window in three ranks. 5 All the doorways and doorposts had square frames.

item.title: 1 Samuel 7
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 7

Samuel Judges Israel

3 Then Samuel told Israel, “If you’re coming back to the LORD with all your heart, put away the foreign gods and the images of Ashtoreth. Direct your heart to serve him only, and he’ll deliver you from the Philistines.” 4 Israel got rid of the images of Baal and Ashtoreth and served the LORD only.

item.title: 2 Chronicles 7
item.description:
2 Chronicles Chapter 7

The Dedication of the Temple

1 When Solomon finished praying, fire came down from the sky and consumed the burnt offering and sacrifices, and the LORD’s glory filled the Temple. 2 The priests couldn’t go in because of it. 3 When the Israelites saw the fire and the LORD’s glory come down on the Temple, they bowed down on the pavement with their faces to the ground in worship and praised him, “He’s truly good; his love endures forever.”

item.title: 2 Kings 7
item.description:
2 Kings Chapter 7

The Siege of Samaria Lifted

1 Elisha said, “Listen to the LORD’s message, ‘Tomorrow about now, .36 bushel 1 seah of fine flour will sell for .4 oz a shekel of silver, and .73 bushel 2 seahs of barley for .4 oz a shekel in Samaria’s gate.’”

2 The royal officer whose hand the king was leaning on answered, “If the LORD opened floodgates in the sky, could that be?”

item.title: 2 Samuel 7
item.description:
2 Samuel Chapter 7

God’s Promise to David

1 The king was living in his palace, and the LORD had given him rest from his enemies. 2 He told Nathan the prophet, “I live in a cedar palace, but God’s Ark sits in tent curtains.”

3 Nathan said, “Do everything you’re thinking; the LORD’s with you.”

item.title: 1 Chronicles 8
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 8

1 BENJAMIN was father of Bela his firstborn, Ashbel second, Aharah third, 2 Nohah fourth, and Rapha fifth.

         3 Bela was father of Addar, Gera, Abihud, 4 Abishua, Naaman, Ahoah, 5 Gera, Shephuphan, and Huram.

         6 These are the sons of Ehud, heads of families among the people in Geba. They were exiled to Manahath: 7 Naaman, Ahijah, and Gera, father of Uzza and Ahihud.

item.title: 1 Kings 8
item.description:
1 Kings Chapter 8

The Ark Brought to the Temple

1-2 In Jerusalem Solomon gathered Israel’s elders, heads of tribes, and leaders of families. They gathered at the festival in the seventh month (Ethanim) to bring up the Ark of the Covenant from The City of David (Zion). 3 The priests picked up the Ark 4 and brought it up with the Tent of Meeting along with the holy items that were in it. The priests and Levites brought them up. 5 King Solomon and the whole community sacrificed more sheep and oxen than you could count. 6 The priests brought the Ark to its place in the inner sanctuary, the Holy of Holies. 7 The cherubs spread out their wings over it and its poles. 8 The poles were long enough for their ends to be visible from the Holy Place in front of the inner sanctuary, but not from the outside. They’re still there. 9 Nothing was in the Ark except the two stone tablets Moses put there at Horeb/Sinai, where the LORD made covenant with Israel. 10 When the priests came out of the Holy Place, a cloud filled the Temple. 11 They couldn’t carry out their service because the LORD’s glory filled the Temple.       1 Kg 8:1-11

item.title: 1 Samuel 8
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 8

Israel Demands a King

1 When Samuel was getting older, he made his sons judges over Israel: 2 Joel his firstborn and Abijah. They judged in Beersheba, 3 but they didn’t follow Samuel’s ways. They pursued dishonest gain, took bribes, and perverted justice.

4 Israel’s elders gathered to Samuel at Ramah, 5 “You’re getting old, and your sons don’t follow your ways. Appoint a king to govern us like the other nations. 6 That displeased Samuel. He prayed to the LORD, 7 and the LORD said,

item.title: 2 Chronicles 8
item.description:
2 Chronicles Chapter 8

Solomon’s Other Activities

1 At the end of the 20 years it took Solomon to build the LORD’s Temple and his own palace, 2 he rebuilt the towns Hiram had given him and settled Israelites in them. 3 Then he captured Hamath-zobah 4 and built Tadmor in the open country and storage cities in Hamath. 5 He built Upper and Lower Beth-horon, fortified towns with walls, gates, and bars. 6 He built Baalath, the storage cities he had, towns for his chariots and cavalry, and everything he delighted to build in Jerusalem, Lebanon, and the rest of the territory he ruled.

item.title: 2 Kings 8
item.description:
2 Kings Chapter 8

The Shunammite’s Land Restored

1 Elisha told the woman whose son he’d revived, “Take your family and live temporarily wherever you can. The LORD has called for a seven-year famine here.” 2 They went to stay with the Philistines. 3 Seven years later, she came back and appealed to the king for her house and farm. 4 He was talking to Gehazi, the man of God’s servant, “Tell me the great things Elisha has done.” 5 As he was telling him how he’d restored life to a boy, she appealed to the king for her house and farm. Gehazi said, “My lord the king, that’s the woman, and that’s her son!” 6 When the king asked her, she told him about it. He appointed an officer to give her back everything, including the crops since she left.            2 Kg 8:1-6

item.title: 2 Samuel 8
item.description:
2 Samuel Chapter 8

David’s Victories

1 After that, David defeated the Philistines and took Methegammah.

2 He defeated Moab and counted them off in lines and made them lie down on the ground. Two lines he counted off to be put to death, and one line he let live. The Moabites served him and brought tribute.

3 David also defeated Hadadezer son of Rehob, king of Zobah, as he went to restore his power at the Euphrates River. 4 He took 1,700 cavalry and 20,000 infantry. He hamstrung the chariot horses but left enough for 100 chariots.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 9
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 9

Those were all descendants of Benjamin.

1 Ch 8:29-40

1 So Israel was enrolled by genealogies. They’re recorded in The Book of the Kings of Israel. Judah was exiled to Babylon because they were unfaithful.

2 The first to live again in their towns were Israel, the priests, the Levites, and the Temple servants. 3 Some descendants of Judah, Benjamin, Ephraim, and Manasseh lived in Jerusalem:

item.title: 1 Kings 9
item.description:
1 Kings Chapter 9

The LORD Appears to Solomon

1 When Solomon had finished building the LORD’s Temple, the king’s palace, and everything he wanted to do, 2 the LORD appeared to him a second time like he did at Gibeon.

3 “I’ve heard your prayer and consecrated this Temple you’ve built by putting my name there permanently. My eyes and heart will always be there. 4 If you’ll follow me like your father did in the integrity of heart and godliness, do everything I tell you, keep my statutes and ordinances, 5 then I’ll make your throne secure over Israel permanently like I promised David, ‘You’ll always have a descendant on Israel’s throne.’

item.title: 1 Samuel 9
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 9

Samuel told Israel, “Every one of you, go home.”

1 Sam 8:10-22

Saul Chosen as King

1 A wealthy man in Benjamin named Kish son of Abiel (son of Zeror son of Becorath son of Aphiah) 2 had a son named Saul, a handsome young man. There wasn’t a man in Israel more handsome than he was. He was head and shoulders taller than everybody.

item.title: 2 Chronicles 9
item.description:
2 Chronicles Chapter 9

The Queen of Sheba Visits Solomon

1 When the queen of Sheba heard about Solomon, she came to Jerusalem to test him with hard questions. She brought a large contingent of camels carrying spices, and lots of gold and precious stones. When she got there, she talked to him about everything on her mind. 2 He answered her questions; there wasn’t anything he couldn’t explain to her. 3 When she’d seen his wisdom, the palace he’d built, 4 the food at his table, the seating of his officials, the attendance by his servers and their attire, his cupbearers and their attire, the stairway he used to go up to the LORD’s Temple, she was breathless,

item.title: 2 Kings 9
item.description:
2 Kings Chapter 9

Jehu Anointed King in Israel

1 Elisha called a man from the school of the prophets,  

“Gird up*, take this flask of olive oil to Ramoth-gilead. 2 Find Jehu son of Jehoshaphat son of Nimshi. Tell him to get up from his companions and take him to a room inside. 3 Pour the flask of olive oil on his head and say, ‘The LORD says, “I’ve anointed you king over Israel.”’ Then open the door and run.”

item.title: 2 Samuel 9
item.description:
2 Samuel Chapter 9

David’s Kindness to Mephibosheth

1 Then David asked if anybody was left from Saul’s house that he could show kindness out of regard for Jonathan. 2 There was a servant named Ziba. They called him to David, and the king said, “You are Ziba?”

“I’m your servant.”

3 “Is anybody left in Saul’s family I can show God’s kindness?”

item.title: 1 Chronicles 10
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 10

The Death of Saul and His Sons

1 The Philistines attacked Israel, and they fled and fell dead on Mount Gilboa. 2 The Philistines overtook Saul and his sons and struck down his sons Jonathan, Abinadab, and Malchi-shua. 3 The battle grew intense against Saul, and the archers wounded him. 4 He told his armor bearer, “Draw your sword and thrust me through so these uncircumcised Philistines don’t come and torture me.” But his armor bearer was too afraid to do it; so Saul took his sword and fell on it. 5 When his armor bearer saw he was dead, he fell on his sword too. 6 So Saul’s whole dynasty died together.

item.title: 1 Kings 10
item.description:
1 Kings Chapter 10

The Queen of Sheba Visits Solomon

1 When the queen of Sheba heard about Solomon’s fame that honored the LORD, she came to Jerusalem to test him with hard questions. 2 She came with a large retinue, and with camels carrying spices, lots of gold, and precious stones. She talked to him about everything that was on her mind. 3 He answered all her questions and explained everything. 4 She saw his wisdom, the palace he’d built, 5 the food on his table, the seating of his officials, the attendance of his waiters and their attire, his cupbearers, and his stairway up to the LORD’s Temple. It took her breath away.

item.title: 1 Samuel 10
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 10

Saul Anointed as King

1 Samuel took a vial of oil, poured it on Saul’s head, and kissed him,

“Hasn’t the LORD anointed you to rule his people? You’ll save Israel from their enemies round about. This will be the sign that the LORD has anointed you ruler over his heritage. 2 When you leave me today, you’ll meet two men by Rachel’s tomb in Benjamin at Zelzah. They’ll say, ‘The donkeys you went to look for they’ve found. Now your father has stopped caring about them and is worrying about you, “What am I going to do about my son?”’ 3 You’ll go on to the Oak of Tabor. Three men going up to God at Bethel will meet you, one carrying three kids, another carrying three loaves of bread, and another carrying a skin of wine. 4 They’ll greet you and give you two loaves of bread; accept them. 5 After that, you’ll come to Gibeah-elohim, where the Philistine outpost is. As you come to the town, you’ll meet a band of prophets coming down from the high place, prophesying behind a harp, tambourine, flute, and lyre. 6 The Spirit of the LORD will come on you powerfully, and you’ll prophesy with them and become another man. 7 When these signs meet you, do whatever you find to do, because God is with you. 8 Go down to Gilgal ahead of me. I’ll come to offer burnt offerings and peace offerings. Wait for a week till I come and show you what to do.”

item.title: 2 Chronicles 10
item.description:
2 Chronicles Chapter 10

The Northern Tribes Revolt

1 Then Rehoboam went to Shechem, because Israel had come to Shechem to make him king. 2 When Jeroboam heard about it, he came back. (He was in Egypt where he’d fled from Solomon.) 3 They sent for him. When Jeroboam and all Israel came, they said to Rehoboam, 4 “Your father made our yoke heavy. Lighten his hard service and the heavy yoke he put on us, and we’ll serve you.”

item.title: 2 Kings 10
item.description:
2 Kings Chapter 10

Ahab’s Family Killed

1 Ahab had 70 sons in Samaria. Jehu wrote letters to Samaria, to the rulers in Jezreel, the elders, and the guardians of Ahab’s children,

2 “When you get this letter, since your master’s sons are with you—as well as the chariots, horses, a fortified city, and weapons— 3 select the best and fittest son, set him on his father’s throne, and fight for your master’s family.”

item.title: 2 Samuel 10
item.description:
2 Samuel Chapter 10

David Defeats the Ammonites

1 The king of the Ammonites died, and his son Hanun became king. 2 David said, “I’ll be kind to Hanun son of Nahash like his father was kind to me.” David sent some ambassadors to console him about his father. But when they got there, 3 the Ammonite officials said to Hanun, “Do you think David is honoring your father because he sent consolers? Hasn’t he sent them to look over the city, spy it out, and overthrow it?” 4 Hanun took David’s ambassadors and shaved off half their beards, cut off their cloaks as high as their hips, and sent them away. 5 When David found out, he sent to meet them because they were really humiliated. He told them to stay at Jericho till their beards grew, and then come back.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 11
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 11

David Anointed King

1 Then Israel gathered to David in Hebron,

“We’re your flesh and bone. 2 When Saul was king, you led out and brought in Israel. The LORD your God said you would shepherd his people and be their leader.”

3 The elders in Israel came to him, and he made a treaty with them in the LORD’s presence. They anointed him king according to the LORD’s message through Samuel.

item.title: 1 Kings 11
item.description:
1 Kings Chapter 11

Solomon’s Wives

1 King Solomon loved many foreign women along with Pharaoh’s daughter: Moabite, Ammonite, Edomite, Sidonian, and Hittite women. 2 They came from the nations the LORD had told the Israelites not to associate with, “They’ll turn your heart away after their gods.” Solomon held fast to them in love. 3 He had 700 wives of royal birth and 300 concubines. 4 When he was old, his wives turned his heart away after other gods. He wasn’t wholly devoted to the LORD like David was. 5 Solomon went after Ashtoreth, goddess of the Sidonians; and Milcom, the detestable idol of the Ammonites. 6 He did what was sin and didn’t follow the LORD faithfully. 7 He built a high place for Chemosh, detestable idol of Moab, on the mountain east of Jerusalem, and for Molech, detestable idol of the Ammonites. 8 He did that for all his foreign wives, whopl burned incense and sacrificed to their gods. 1 Kg 11:1-8

item.title: 1 Samuel 11
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 11

Saul Defeats the Amonites

1 Nahash the Ammonite went up to lay siege to Jabesh-gilead. The men there said to him, “Make a treaty with us, and we’ll serve you.” 

2 But he said, “I’ll make one with you on this condition: I’ll gouge out your right eyes so you’ll be a disgrace in Israel.”

item.title: 2 Chronicles 11
item.description:
2 Chronicles Chapter 11

Shemaiah’s Prophecy

1 When Rehoboam got to Jerusalem, he gathered 180,000 soldiers from Judah and Benjamin to fight Israel to restore the kingdom to himself. 2 But a message from the LORD came to Shemaiah, the man of God,  

                  3“Tell Rehoboam and all Israel in Judah and Benjamin, 4 ‘The LORD says, “Don’t fight your kin. Go back home. I’m going to let this happen.”’”

item.title: 2 Kings 11
item.description:
2 Kings Chapter 11

Queen Athaliah and Joash

1 When Athaliah, Ahaziah’s mother, saw her son was dead, she destroyed the other royal offspring. 2 But Jehosheba, King Joram’s daughter, Ahaziah’s sister, stole Ahaziah’s son Joash from the king’s sons they were killing. She hid him with his nurse in a bedroom, so he wasn’t killed. 3 She hid him in the LORD’s Temple for 6 years while Athaliah ruled the country.

item.title: 2 Samuel 11
item.description:
2 Samuel Chapter 11

David and Bathsheba

1 In the spring when kings normally go to war, David sent Joab and Israel’s army to destroy the Ammonites and lay siege to Rabbah. David stayed in Jerusalem.

2 When evening came, David got up from his bed and walked around on the roof of his palace. From the roof he saw a woman taking a bath; she was beautiful. 3 David sent and asked about her. Someone said, “Isn’t this Bathsheba, daughter of Eliam, wife of Uriah the Hittite?” 4 David sent messengers and took her. When she came to him, he slept with her. When she’d purified herself, she went home. 5 She got pregnant and sent and told David.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 12
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 12

Warriors Join David

1 These are men that came over to David at Ziklag, while he still couldn’t move around freely because of Saul. They were among the elite soldiers that helped him during war. 2 They used bows. With either hand they could sling stones and shoot arrows. They were Saul’s relatives from Benjamin. 3 The chief was Ahiezer, then Joash, sons of Shemaah the Gibeathite; Jeziel and Pelet, sons of Azmaveth; Beracah and Jehu the Anathothite; 4 Ishmaiah the Gibeonite, an elite soldier among the 30 and a leader over them. Then came Jeremiah, Jahaziel, Johanan, Jozabad the Gederathite, 5 Eluzai, Jerimoth, Bealiah, Shemariah, Shephatiah the Haruphite; 6 Elkanah, Isshiah, Azarel, Joezer, Jashobeam, the Korahites; 7 Joelah and Zebadiah, sons of Jerobam of Gedor.

item.title: 1 Kings 12
item.description:
1 Kings Chapter 12

The Northern Tribes Rebel Against Rehoboam

1 Rehoboam went to Shechem because all Israel had come there to make him king. 2 Jeroboam heard about it in Egypt. ( 3 They sent and called him.) Jeroboam and all Israel came and told Rehoboam, 4 “Your father made our yoke hard. Lighten his hard service and his heavy yoke, and we’ll serve you.” 5 He said, “Leave for three days, then come back.” So they left.

item.title: 1 Samuel 12
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 12

Samuel’s Farewell Address

1 Then Samuel told Israel,

“I’ve listened to you and appointed a king. 2 Now here’s the king living among you, but I’m getting old and gray; my sons are with you. I’ve lived among you since I was young. 3 Here I am. Accuse me in the presence of the LORD and his anointed. Whose ox or donkey have I taken? Who’ve I defrauded or oppressed? Who’ve I taken a bribe from to blind me? I’ll return it to you.”

item.title: 2 Chronicles 12
item.description:
2 Chronicles Chapter 12

Shishak [Shoshenq I] Invades Judah

1 When Rehoboam’s kingdom got secure and strong, he and Israel turned away from the Law of the LORD. 2 In his fifth year, because they weren’t faithful to the LORD, Shishak, king of Egypt, came up against Jerusalem 3 with 1,200 chariots and 60,000 cavalry. The Lybians, Sukkites, and Nubians came with him without number. 4 He captured the fortified towns in Judah and advanced to Jerusalem. 5 Then Shemaiah the prophet came to Rehoboam and Judah’s officials that had fled to Jerusalem to escape Shishak, “The LORD says you’ve left him, so he’s left you to Shishak.” 6 The officials of Israel and the king humbled themselves, “The LORD is good.”

item.title: 2 Kings 12
item.description:
2 Kings Chapter 12

Joash Repairs the Temple

1 In the seventh year of Jehu, Jehoash became king, and ruled for 40 years in Jerusalem. His mother was Zibiah of Beersheba. 2 His whole life he did what was good like Jehoiada instructed him, 3 except he didn’t take away the high places. The people still sacrificed and burned incense on them.

4-5 Jehoash told the priests,

item.title: 2 Samuel 12
item.description:
2 Samuel Chapter 12

Nathan Rebukes David

1 Then the LORD sent Nathan to David to say,

“Two men were in a town, one rich, the other poor. 2 The rich man had lots of flocks and herds, 3 but the poor man had nothing but one little ewe lamb that he’d bought and raised. It grew up with him and his children; it used to eat his bread and drink from his cup and lie in his lap; it was like a daughter to him. 4 A traveler came to the rich man, and he wasn’t willing to take one of his own flock or herd to fix a meal for him. He took the poor man’s lamb and fixed it.”

item.title: 1 Chronicles 13
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 13

The Ark Brought from Kiriath-Jearim

1 Then David consulted with every commander of thousands and hundreds, 2 and told the whole assembly,

“If it seems good to you and if the LORD our God wills, let’s send around to our relatives that are still in Israel, as well as to the priests and Levites that are with them in their towns and pastures, to have them meet with us. 3 Let’s bring the Ark back. We neglected it in Saul’s time.”

item.title: 1 Kings 13
item.description:
1 Kings Chapter 13

A Prophet Denounces Jeroboam

1 A man of God went from Judah to Bethel at the LORD’s command while Jeroboam was standing by the altar to burn incense. 2 He cried out against the altar by a message from the LORD,

“Altar, altar, the LORD says, ‘A son named Josiah will be born among David’s descendants. He’ll sacrifice on you the priests of the high places who burn incense on you. He’ll burn human bones on you.’”

item.title: 1 Samuel 13
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 13

1 Saul was 40 when he began to rule and ruled for 32 years. 2 He chose 3,000 men from Israel, 2,000 to be with him in Michmash and the hill country around Bethel and 1,000 to be with Jonathan at Gibeah in Benjamin. He sent the rest home. 3 Jonathan struck the Philistine garrison in Geba, and the Philistines heard about it. Then Saul blew the ram’s horn all over the land and said, “Hebrews, listen.” 4 All Israel heard that Saul had struck the garrison and that Israel had become repulsive to the Philistines. So Saul called the people to Gilgal.

item.title: 2 Chronicles 13
item.description:
2 Chronicles Chapter 13

Abijah Rules Judah

1 In the eighteenth year of King Jeroboam, Abijah became king in Judah. 2 He ruled for 3 years in Jerusalem. His mother was Micaiah, daughter of Uriel of Gibeah. War arose between Abijah and Jeroboam. 3 Abijah entered the battle with 400,000 trained soldiers; Jeroboam drew up in battle formation against him with 800,000 trained soldiers. 2 Ch 13:1-3

item.title: 2 Kings 13
item.description:
2 Kings Chapter 13

Jehoahaz Rules in Israel

1 In the twenty-third year of Joash/Jehoash son of Ahaziah, king of Judah, Jehoahaz son of Jehu became king of Israel in Samaria and ruled for 17 years. 2 He followed the sins of JeroboamI that got Israel to sin. 3 The LORD’s anger flared; he kept delivering them to Hazael, king of Aram, and Benhadad his son. 4 Jehoahaz asked for the LORD’s help and he listened to him, because he saw the oppression by the king of Aram. 5 The LORD gave Israel a deliverer, and they escaped from under the Arameans and lived like before. 6 But they didn’t turn away from the sins of JeroboamI that he got Israel to sin; the Asherah pole remained standing in Samaria. 7 The Arameans reduced Jehoahaz’s army to not more than 50 cavalry, 10 chariots, and 10,000 infantry, and made them like dust at threshing time. 8 The rest of the acts of Jehoahaz and the strength of his rule are recorded in The Chronicles of the Kings of Israel. 9 He died and they buried him in Samaria. His son Joash became king.                                                                                                                   2 Kg 13:1-9

item.title: 2 Samuel 13
item.description:
2 Samuel Chapter 13

Amnon and Tamar

1 David’s son Absalom had a beautiful sister named Tamar, and David’s son Ammon was in love with her. 2 Amnon was so frustrated because of his sister that he made himself ill, because she was a virgin and it seemed hard to do anything to her. 3 But Amnon had a friend Jonadab son of Shimeah, David’s brother, and Jonadab was a shrewd fellow.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 14
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 14

David’s Wives and Children

1 Hiram, king of Tyre, sent ambassadors to David with cedar trees, masons, and carpenters, to build him a palace. 2 David saw that the LORD had made him secure as king over Israel, and that his kingdom was highly regarded for Israel’s sake. 3 He took more wives at Jerusalem and had more sons and daughters. 4 His children born in Jerusalem were Shammua, Shobab, Nathan, Solomon, 5 Ibhar, Elishua, Elpelet, 6 Nogah, Nepheg, Japhia, 7 Elishama, Beeliada, and Eliphelet. 1 Ch 14:1-7

item.title: 1 Kings 14
item.description:
1 Kings Chapter 14

Ahijah’s Prophecy Against Jeroboam

1 At that time, Abijah, Jeroboam’sI son, got sick. 2 Jeroboam told his wife, 

         “Disguise yourself and go to Shiloh. Ahijah the prophet is there who said I’d be king. 3 Take ten loaves of bread, some cakes, and a jar of honey. He’ll tell you what will happen to the boy.”

item.title: 1 Samuel 14
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 14

Jonathan Defeats the Philistines

1 One day Jonathan said to his young armor bearer, “Let’s go over to the Philistine outpost on the other side.” He didn’t tell his father, 2 who was staying with about 600 men on the outskirts of Gibeah around the pomegranate tree in Migron. 3 Among Saul’s men was Ahijah wearing an ephod—son of Ahitub (Ichabod’s brother) son of Phinehas son of Eli the LORD’s priest at Shiloh. The people didn’t know Jonathan had gone. 4 Between the passes Jonathan was planning to use to cross over to the Philistines’ outpost, there were steep cliffs on two sides named Bozez and Seneh. 5 One went up to the north toward Michmash, the other one on the south toward Geba.                                                                                                                                                                            1 Sam 14:1-5

item.title: 2 Chronicles 14
item.description:
2 Chronicles Chapter 14

1 When Abijah died, they buried him in The City of David, and his son Asa became king. The country had peace for 10 years.  

Asa Rules Judah

2 Asa did what was good. 3 He removed the foreign altars and high places, tore down the scared pillars, cut down the Asherah poles, 4 and commanded Judah to look to the LORD God of their ancestors and to observe the Law and the commandments. 5 He removed the high places and incense altars from the towns in Judah. 6 He built fortified towns in Judah since the LORD had given the country rest. 7 He said to Judah,

item.title: 2 Kings 14
item.description:
2 Kings Chapter 14

Amaziah Rules in Judah

1 In the second year of Joash/Jehoash son of Joahaz, king of Israel, Amaziah son of Joash became king of Judah. 2 He was 25 and ruled for 29 years in Jerusalem. His mother was Jehoaddin of Jerusalem. 3 He did what was good, but not like David did. He did what Joash his father had done, 4 but he didn’t take away the high places. The people still sacrificed and burned incense on them. 5 As soon as the kingdom was firmly in hand, he executed the officials that had assassinated his father. 6 He didn’t execute their sons, as the Law said that the LORD commanded Moses, “Don’t execute parents for offspring or offspring for parents. Execute each person for that person’s sin.” 7 He killed 10,000 Edomites in the Valley of Salt, took Sela, and changed its name to Joktheel to this day.                                      2 Kg 14:1-7

item.title: 2 Samuel 14
item.description:
2 Samuel Chapter 14

Absalom Returns to Jerusalem

1 Joab sensed that the king yearned for Absalom. 2 So he sent to Tekoa and brought a wise woman, and told her,

“Act like you’re in mourning. Put on mourning clothes, don’t anoint yourself with oil. Act like a woman that’s been mourning for the dead for many days. 3 Then go to the king and say such and such.”

item.title: 1 Chronicles 15
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 15

The Ark Brought to Jerusalem

1 David built houses for himself in The City of David and prepared a place for the Ark of God and pitched a tent for it. 2 He said, “Don’t anybody carry the Ark but Levites. The LORD chose them to carry it and serve him permanently.” 3 He gathered Israel to Jerusalem to bring the Ark up to the place he’d prepared. 4 He gathered the descendants of Aaron and the Levites:

item.title: 1 Kings 15
item.description:
1 Kings Chapter 15

Abijam Rules in Judah

1 In the eighteenth year of King Jeroboam son of Nebat, Abijam began to reign over Judah 2 and ruled for 3 years in Jerusalem. His mother was Maacah daughter of Abishalom. 3 He did the sins his father did. His heart wasn’t wholly true to the LORD like David’s was. 4 But for David’s sake the LORD gave him a lamp in Jerusalem, set up his descendant after him, and made Jerusalem secure. 5 He did it because David did what was right and didn’t deviate from anything the LORD commanded him his whole life—except in the matter about Uriah the Hittite. [ 6 ] 7 The rest of the acts of Abijam are recorded in The Chronicles of the Kings of Judah. 8 He died and they buried him in The City of David. His son Asa became king.

item.title: 1 Samuel 15
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 15

The LORD Rejects Saul

1 Then Samuel told Saul,

“The LORD of Hosts sent me to anoint you king over his people. 2 He says, ‘I’m going to settle up Amalek for opposing Israel on their way up from Egypt. 3 Strike him and destroy everything he has. Don’t spare him. Put to death man, woman, child, infant, ox, sheep, camel, and donkey.’”

item.title: 2 Chronicles 15
item.description:
2 Chronicles Chapter 15

Asa’s Reforms

1 The Spirit of God came on Azariah son of Oded, 2 and he went out to meet Asa,

“Listen, Asa, Judah, and Benjamin, the LORD is with you when you’re with him. If you look for him, he’ll let you find him. If you leave him, he’ll leave you. 3 For a long time Israel was without the true God, without a teaching priest, without law. 4 But in their distress they turned to the LORD and looked for him, and he let them find him. 5 In those times, there wasn’t any peace to the ones who traveled; disturbances troubled the people in many countries. 6 Nation crushed nation, city crushed city, because God distressed them with all kinds of problems. 7 But you, be strong. Don’t lose courage; there’s reward for your work.”

item.title: 2 Kings 15
item.description:
2 Kings Chapter 15

Azariah Rules in Judah

1 In the twenty-seventh year of JeroboamII, king of Israel, Azariah/Uzziah son of Amaziah became king in Judah. 2 He was 16 and ruled for 52 years in Jerusalem. His mother was Jecoliah of Jerusalem. 3 He did what was good like his father, 4 but he didn’t take away the high places. The people still sacrificed and burned incense on them. 5 The LORD made him a leper till he died. He lived in a separate house while his son Jotham managed the palace and judged the people. 6 The rest of the acts of Azariah are recorded in The Chronicles of the Kings of Judah. 7 He died and they buried him in The City of David, and Jotham became king.                                              2 Kg 15:1-7

item.title: 2 Samuel 15
item.description:
2 Samuel Chapter 15

Absalom’s Conspiracy

1 After that, Absalom provided himself a chariot, horses, and 50 men to run ahead of him. 2 He’d get up early and stand by the road to the gate. When anybody had a suit to come to the king for judgment, Absalom would call to him and say,

“What city are you from?”

He’d say, “I’m from a tribe in Israel.”

item.title: 1 Chronicles 16
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 16

1 They put the Ark in the tent David had pitched for it and offered burnt offerings and peace offerings to God. 2 When David had finished offering them, he blessed the people in the LORD’s name. 3 To every man and woman in Israel, he passed out a loaf of bread, a helping of meat, and a raisin cake.

item.title: 1 Kings 16
item.description:
1 Kings Chapter 16

1 A message from the LORD came to Jehu son of Hanani against Baasha:

2 “Since I lifted you up from the dust to lead Israel, you’ve done what Jeroboam did and got Israel to do and made me angry by their sins. 3 I’ll consume you and your family and make it like Jeroboam’s. 4 Dogs will eat any relative that dies in the city; birds will eat any that die in the field.”

item.title: 1 Samuel 16
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 16

David Anointed King

1 The LORD said to Samuel,

“How long are you going to grieve over Saul? I’ve set him aside from being king. Fill your horn with olive oil and go to Jesse of Bethlehem. I’ve selected a king from his sons.”

2 Samuel said, “How can I go there? When Saul hears about it, he’ll kill me.”

item.title: 2 Chronicles 16
item.description:
2 Chronicles Chapter 16

Asa’s Final Years

1 In the thirty-sixth year of Asa’s reign, Baasha, king of Israel, came up against Judah and fortified Ramah to prevent anyone from going out or coming in to Asa. 2 Asa brought out silver and gold from the treasuries of the LORD’s Temple and the king’s palace, and sent them to Benhadad, king of Aram, who lived in Damascus, and said, 3 “Let’s make a treaty between us, like the one between our fathers. I’ve sent you silver and gold; go break your treaty with Baasha so he’ll withdraw from me.” 4 Benhadad listened and sent his armies against the towns in Israel. They conquered Ijon, Dan, Abel-maim, and the store towns in Naphtali. 5 When Baasha heard about it, he stopped fortifying Ramah. 6 Then King Asa brought Judah, and they carried away the stones and timber from Ramah that Baasha had been using, and with them Asa fortified Geba and Mizpah.   2 Ch 16:1-6

item.title: 2 Kings 16
item.description:
2 Kings Chapter 16

Ahaz Rules in Judah

1 In the seventeenth year of Pekah, Ahaz son of Jotham became king. 2 He was 20 and ruled for 16 years in Jerusalem. He didn’t do what was right like David did. 3 He lived like the kings in Israel, and even burned his son as an offering like the horrible things the nations did that the LORD had originally driven out. 4 He sacrificed and burned incense on the high places and hills under every green tree.

item.title: 2 Samuel 16
item.description:
2 Samuel Chapter 16

David and Ziba

1 When David had gone a little past the summit, Ziba, Mephibosheth’s steward, met him with a couple saddled donkeys. They were carrying 200 loaves of bread, 100 clusters of raisins, 100 summer fruits, and a skin of wine.

2 The king asked, “Why do you have these?”

“The donkeys are for the king’s family to ride. The bread and summer fruit are for the young men to eat. The wine is for anybody to drink that’s become exhausted in the country.”

item.title: 1 Chronicles 17
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 17

The LORD’s Covenant with David

1 While David was living in his palace, he told Nathan the prophet, “I’m living in a cedar palace, but the LORD’s Ark is under curtains.”

2 Nathan said, “Do what’s in your mind. God is with you.”                                    

3 That same night, the message from God came to him,

item.title: 1 Kings 17
item.description:
1 Kings Chapter 17

Elijah Predicts a Drought

1 Elijah the Tishbite, a settler in Gilead, told Ahab,

“As the LORD God of Israel lives that I serve, there won’t be dew or rain the next several years except when I say so.”

2 A message from the LORD came to him,

item.title: 1 Samuel 17
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 17

David and Goliath

1 The Philistines gathered their army for battle at Socoh in Judah. They camped between Socoh and Azekah in Ephes-dammim. 2 Saul and Israel came together, camped in the Valley of Elah, and drew up in battle formation to meet them. 3 The Philistines stood on the hill on one side of the valley, and Israel stood on the hill on the other side. 4 A champion from Gath named Goliath came out from the Philistine camp. He was 9ft 9 6 cubits and a span, 5 and wore a bronze helmet and scale armor that weighed 125 lbs 5,000 shekels. 6 He had bronze greaves on his shins and a bronze javelin slung between his shoulders. 7 His spear shaft was like a weaver’s beam; its spearhead weighed 15 lbs 600 shekels of iron. His shield bearer walked ahead of him. 8 He stood and shouted to the ranks of Israel,

item.title: 2 Chronicles 17
item.description:
2 Chronicles Chapter 17

Jehoshaphat Rules Judah

1 His son Jehoshaphat became king and secured his position against Israel. 2 He placed troops in Judah’s fortified towns and set garrisons in Judah and the towns in Ephraim his father had captured. 3 The LORD was with him because he followed his ancestor David’s earlier days and didn’t look to the images of Baal. 4 He sought his father’s God, followed his commandments, and didn’t do like Israel. 5 The LORD secured the kingdom under him, Judah brought tributes to him, and he had great wealth and honor. 6 He took pride in the ways of the LORD and removed again the high places and Asherah poles from Judah.

item.title: 2 Kings 17
item.description:
2 Kings Chapter 17

Hoshea Last King of Israel

1 In the twelfth year of Ahaz, Hoshea son of Elah became king of Israel in Samaria and ruled for 9 years. 2 He did evil, but not as bad as the previous kings of Israel. 3 Shalmaneser, king of Assyria, attacked him, and he had to pay tribute to Assyria. 4 But the king of Assyria discovered a plot he was laying. He’d sent ambassadors to So, king of Egypt, and withheld the annual tribute to Assyria; so Shalmaneser shut him up bound in prison. 5 Then he invaded the whole country and went up to Samaria and laid siege to it for 3 years. 6 In the ninth year of Hoshea, the king of Assyria conquered the city and took Israel into exile in Assyria. He settled them in Halah and Habor on the Gozan River and in towns of the Medes.    2 Kg 17:1-6

item.title: 2 Samuel 17
item.description:
2 Samuel Chapter 17

1 In addition, Ahithophel told Absalom,

“Let me choose 12,000 men to pursue David tonight. 2 I’ll attack him while he’s exhausted and terrify him. The people with him will run. I’ll only strike down the king 3 and bring the people back to you. Everybody’s return depends on the man you’re looking for. Then the people will be at peace.”

item.title: 1 Chronicles 18
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 18

David’s Military Victories

1 After that, David defeated the Philistines and took Gath and its surrounding towns. 2 He defeated Moab and they brought tribute. 3 He defeated Hadadezer, king of Zobah, as far as Hamath and secured his rule all the way to the Euphrates River. 4 He took 1,000 chariots, 7,000 cavalry, and 20,000 foot soldiers. He hamstrung the chariot horses but reserved enough for 100 chariots.

item.title: 1 Kings 18
item.description:
1 Kings Chapter 18

Elijah Confronts Ahab

1 In the third year, a message from the LORD came to Elijah, “Present yourself to Ahab, and I’ll send rain.” 2 So he went to Ahab. The famine was severe in Samaria. 3 Ahab called Obadiah, who managed the palace. (Obadiah respected the LORD greatly. 4 When Jezebel was destroying the LORD’s prophets, he hid 100 of them by 50s in a cave and provided them bread and water.)

item.title: 1 Samuel 18
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 18

Saul’s Jealousy of David

         1 When he’d finished talking to Saul, Jonathan was drawn to David and loved him as himself. 2 Saul took him that day and didn’t let him go back home. 3 Jonathan made a pact with him because he loved him as himself. 4 Jonathan took off his robe and gave it to him, along with his armor, sword, bow, and belt. 5 So David went out wherever Saul sent him and was successful. Saul set him over the fighting men. The people and Saul liked that arrangement.

item.title: 2 Chronicles 18
item.description:
2 Chronicles Chapter 18

Jehoshaphat and Ahab

1 Jehoshaphat had great wealth and honor, and allied himself by marriage with Ahab. 2 Some years later he went down to visit Ahab in Samaria. Ahab slaughtered lots of sheep and oxen for him and the people with him, and induced him to go up against Ramoth-gilead. 3 He asked Jehoshaphat, “Will you go with me against Ramoth-gilead?”

item.title: 2 Kings 18
item.description:
2 Kings Chapter 18

Hezekiah Rules in Judah

1 In the third year of Hoshea, Hezekiah son of Ahaz became king in Judah. 2 He was 25 and ruled for 29 years in Jerusalem. His mother was Abi daughter of Zechariah. 3 He did what was right like David did. 4 He removed the high places, broke down the sacred pillars, cut down the Asherah poles, and broke in pieces the bronze “serpent” Moses had made, because the Israelites had been burning incense to it; they called it Nehushtan. 5 He trusted in the LORD God of Israel so that no king in Judah was like him before or after. 6 He held to the LORD and kept the commandments he gave Moses. 7 The LORD was with him and he prospered wherever he went. He revolted against the king of Assyria 8 and defeated the Philistines all the way to Gaza and its territory, from outpost to fortified city.

item.title: 2 Samuel 18
item.description:
2 Samuel Chapter 18

The Death of Absalom

1 Then David counted his people and set over them commanders of thousands and hundreds. 2 He sent out the troops, a third each under Joab, Abishai, and Ittai. He told them, “I’ll go out with you too.”

 3 They said,

“You shouldn’t go out. If we flee, they won’t care even if half of us die. You’re worth 10,000 of us. It’s better for you to be ready to help us from town.”

item.title: 1 Chronicles 19
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 19

David Defeats the Ammonites and Syrians

1 After this, Nahash, king of the Ammonites, died and his son became king. 2 David said, “I’ll be kind to Hanun because his father was kind to me.” He sent ambassadors to console him about his father. 3 But the officials in Ammon told Hanun, “Do you think David is honoring your father by sending ambassadors to comfort you? Haven’t they come to spy out the land to overthrow it?” 4 Hanun took David’s ambassadors and shaved them, cut off their cloaks as high as their hips, and sent them away. 5 Some people came and told David about the men. He sent to meet them because they were humiliated. He told them to stay in Jericho till their beards grew back; then come home.

item.title: 1 Kings 19
item.description:
1 Kings Chapter 19

Elijah Flees to Sinai

1 Ahab told Jezebel what Elijah had done, and how he’d killed the prophets with the sword. 2 She sent a messenger to Elijah, “The gods do so to me and more, if I don’t make your life like theirs by tomorrow at this time.” 3 He fled for his life to Beersheba in Judah and left his attendant there. 4 He went on a day’s journey into the open country and sat down under a juniper tree, “That’s enough, LORD. Take my life. I’m no better than my ancestors.” 5 He lay down and slept under the tree, and an angel touched him, “Get up and eat.” 6 He looked and at his head was a loaf of bread baked on hot stones and a jug of water. He ate and lay back down. 7 An angel of the LORD came a second time and touched him, “Get up and eat some more, or the trip will be too much for you.” 8 He ate and on the strength of that food went for 40 days to Horeb/Sinai, the mountain of God. 1 Kg 19:1-8

item.title: 1 Samuel 19
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 19

Saul Tries to Kill David

1 Saul told Jonathan and his servants to put David to death. But Jonathan really delighted in David. 2 He told David,

“My father is trying to get you killed. Be on guard in the morning. Hide in a private place. 3 I’ll go out and stand beside him in the field where you are and talk about you. If I find out anything out, I’ll tell you.”

item.title: 2 Chronicles 19
item.description:
2 Chronicles Chapter 19

Jehoshaphat Appoints Judges

1 Jehoshaphat went back safely to his palace in Jerusalem. 2 Jehu son of Hanani the seer went out to meet him and said,

“Should you help the sinful, love the ones that hate the LORD, and bring his wrath on you? 3 But there’s some good in you: you’ve removed the Asherah poles from the country and set your heart to look to God.”

item.title: 2 Kings 19
item.description:
2 Kings Chapter 19

Jerusalem’s Deliverance Foretold

            1 When Hezekiah heard it, he tore his clothes, covered himself with burlap, and went into the LORD’s Temple. 2 He sent Eliakim with Shebna and the elders of the people, covered with burlap, to the prophet Isaiah son of Amoz. 3 They told him,

“Hezekiah says, ‘Today is a time of distress, rebuke, and rejection. Babies have come to term, but there’s no strength to deliver them. 4 Maybe the LORD your God will hear the message from the Rabshakeh that the king of Assyria has sent to disparage the living God. Maybe he’ll rebuke what he’s heard. Pray for the remnant that’s left.’” [5]

item.title: 2 Samuel 19
item.description:
2 Samuel Chapter 19

            1 Then Joab heard, “The king is crying and mourning for Absalom.” 2 The victory that day turned into mourning because those who heard about it said, “The king is grieving for his son.” 3 So the people slipped into the city like humiliated people sneak away from battle. 4 The king covered his face and cried out, “Absalom! My son! My son!”

item.title: 1 Chronicles 20
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 20

The Capture of Rabbah

1 In the spring, when kings go to war, Joab led out the army and ravaged the country of the Ammonites and laid siege to Rabbah. David stayed in Jerusalem. Joab attacked Rabbah and overthrew it. 2 Then David went to Rabbah and took the crown off their king’s head, and found that it weighed 75½ lbs a talent of gold, and was set with precious stones. They placed it on David’s head, and he brought out a really large amount of spoil from the city. 3 He brought the people out, and cut them with saws, sharp instruments, and axes. He did that to all the Ammonite towns. Then he and the people came back to Jerusalem.

item.title: 1 Kings 20
item.description:
1 Kings Chapter 20

Ahab’s War with Syria

1 Benhadad, king of Aram, gathered his army, 32 kings with horses and chariots. He went up and laid siege to Samaria and attacked it. 2 He sent ambassadors to Ahab in the city, “Benhadad says, 3 ‘Your silver and gold are mine. Your most beautiful wives and children are mine too.’”  

4 The king of Israel said, “It’s according to your message, my lord king. I and everything I have are yours.”

item.title: 1 Samuel 20
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 20

Jonathan Warns David

1 Then David fled from Naioth in Ramah and came to Jonathan, “What have I done? What am I guilty of? What sin did I commit to make your father try to kill me?”

2 He said,

“Far from it! You’re not going to die. My father doesn’t do anything big or small without letting me know. Why would he keep that from me? It’s not so.”

item.title: 2 Chronicles 20
item.description:
2 Chronicles Chapter 20

Jehoshaphat Defeats Moab and Ammon

1 After that, the Moabites, Ammonites, and some Meunites came to attack Jehoshaphat. 2 Some people brought him a report, “A large army is coming out of Edom from the other side of the sea. They’re in Hazazon-tamar (En-gedi).” 3 He was afraid, set himself to look to the LORD, and declared a fast throughout Judah. 4 Judah gathered from all the towns to look to the LORD for help.     2 Ch 20:1-4

item.title: 2 Kings 20
item.description:
2 Kings Chapter 20

Hezekiah’s Illness

1 At that time, Hezekiah became deathly ill, and Isaiah came to tell him, “The LORD says to set your house in order; you’re going to die.”

2 He turned his face to the wall and prayed, 3 “Remember how I’ve lived in your presence honestly with all my heart. I’ve done what you considered good.” He cried bitterly. 4 Before Isaiah had left the middle court, a message came to him,

item.title: 2 Samuel 20
item.description:
2 Samuel Chapter 20

Sheba Rebels Against David

1 A troublemaker happened to be there—Sheba son of Bicri, a Benjamite. He blew the ram’s horn and said,

            “We don’t have any part with David

                        or any inheritance in Jesse’s son.

            Everybody, go home, Israel!”

2 The men from Israel deserted David and followed Sheba. But Judah stayed firmly committed to their king and escorted him from the Jordan to Jerusalem.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 21
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 21

David’s Ill-Advised Census

1 Satan stood up against Israel and tempted David to take a census. 2 David told Joab and the commanders of the troops,

“Go count Israel from Dan to Beersheba and bring me the results so I can know how many there are.”

 3 Joab said, “May the LORD add to his people 100 times as many as they are! But my lord the king, aren’t they all serving you? Why do you want to know this? Why bring guilt on Israel?”

item.title: 1 Kings 21
item.description:
1 Kings Chapter 21

Naboth’s Vineyard

1 After this, Naboth the Jezreelite had a vineyard in Jezreel beside King Ahab’s palace. 2 Ahab spoke to him about it, “Give me your vineyard so I can use it for a vegetable garden; it’s close to my palace. I’ll trade you a better vineyard for it.”

3 But Naboth said, “The LORD forbid me from giving you my ancestors’ inheritance.”

item.title: 1 Samuel 21
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 21

He got up and left, and Jonathan went into town.

1 Sam 20:35-42

David and the Bread of the Presence

1 Then David went to Nob to Ahimelech the priest. He came cringing to meet David, “Why are you by yourself?”

2 David told Ahimelech, “The king commissioned me with a matter, ‘Don’t let anybody know about it. I’ve directed my young men where to meet me later.’ 3 What do you have on hand? Give me five loaves of bread or whatever you can find.”

item.title: 2 Chronicles 21
item.description:
2 Chronicles Chapter 21

Jehoram Rules Judah

1 Jehoshaphat died, and they buried him in The City of David. His son Jehoram became king. 2 His brothers were Azariah, Jehiel, Zechariah, Azariyahu, Michael, and Shephatiah. 3 Their father had given them many gifts of silver, gold, and other precious things along with fortified towns in Judah. But he gave the kingdom to Jehoram, his firstborn.

item.title: 2 Kings 21
item.description:
2 Kings Chapter 21

Manasseh Rules in Judah

1 Manasseh was 12 and ruled for 55 years in Jerusalem. His mother was Hephzibah. 2 He did evil like nations the LORD originally expelled. 3 He rebuilt the high places Hezekiah had destroyed, erected altars for Baal, and set up an Asherah pole like Ahab, king of Israel, had done. He worshiped and served the host of heaven, 4 built altars in the LORD’s Temple where the LORD had said, “I’ll identify myself with Jerusalem.” 5 He built altars for the host of heaven in the two courts of the LORD’s Temple. 6 He burned his son as an offering, practiced witchcraft, used sorcery, and dealt with mediums and spiritists. He did so much evil and made the LORD angry. 7 He put a carved image of Asherah in the Temple where the LORD told David and his son Solomon,

item.title: 2 Samuel 21
item.description:
2 Samuel Chapter 21

David Avenges the Gibeonites

1 In David’s time, a famine came that lasted three years. He sought the LORD. The LORD said, “It’s because Saul and his bloody family murdered the people in Gibeon.” 2 (The inhabitants of Gibeon weren’t part of Israel but a remnant of the Amorites. The Israelites had made a treaty with them, but Saul had intended to kill them off in his zeal for Israel and Judah.) The king called them,

item.title: 1 Chronicles 22
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 22

David’s Preparations for the Temple

2 David issued an order to gather the foreigners in Israel and set stonecutters to dress stones for God’s Temple. 3 He provided large stores of iron for making nails for the doors in the gates and for clamps, more bronze than could be weighed, 4 and cedar logs without number. The Sidonians and Tyrians brought him large supplies of cedar timber. 5 He said,

item.title: 1 Kings 22
item.description:
1 Kings Chapter 22

Ahab and the False Prophets

1 Three years passed without war between Aram and Israel. 2 In the third year, Jehoshaphat, king of Judah, came down to visit the king of Israel. 3 The king of Israel said to his officials, “Do you know that Ramoth-gilead belongs to us, and we’re still not doing anything to take it back from the king of Aram?” 4 He asked Jehoshaphat, “Will you go with me to battle at Ramoth-gilead?”

item.title: 1 Samuel 22
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 22

David at the Case of Adullam

            1 David left and got to the cave near Adullam. When his brothers and father’s family heard, they came down. 2 Everybody in distress, in debt, or disheartened gathered to him till became the captain of about 400 men.

3 He went from there to Mizpah in Moab and said to the king, “Please let my parents stay with you till I know what God will do for me.” 4 He left them with the king; they stayed with him the whole time David was in the fortress. 5 The prophet Gad told David, “Don’t stay in the fortress. Go to Judah.” So David went to the Forest of Hereth.

item.title: 2 Chronicles 22
item.description:
2 Chronicles Chapter 22

Ahaziah Rules Judah

1 Then the people in Jerusalem made Ahaziah/Jehoahaz, his youngest son, king. The band of men that came with the Arabs to the camp had killed the older sons. 2 Ahaziah was 22 and ruled for 1 year in Jerusalem. His mother was Athaliah, granddaughter of Omri. 3 To his destruction, he also followed Ahab’s practices because his mother gave him bad advice. 4 He sinned in 5 going with Jehoram son of Ahab to fight Hazael, king of Aram, at Ramoth-gilead. But the Arameans wounded Joram/Jehoram. 6 He came back to heal in Jezreel from his wounds at Ramah. Ahaziah son of Joram/Jehoram king of Judah went down to see Joram/Jehoram in Jezreel because he was wounded.       2 Ch 22:1-6

item.title: 2 Kings 22
item.description:
2 Kings Chapter 22

The Book of the Law Found

1 Josiah was 8 when he became king and ruled for 31 years in Jerusalem. His mother was Jedidah daughter of Adaiah from Bozkath. 2 He did what was good and followed the ways of David.

3 In the eighteenth year of King Josiah, he sent Shaphan son of Azaliah son of Meshullam, the secretary, to the LORD’s Temple,

item.title: 2 Samuel 22
item.description:
2 Samuel Chapter 22

David’s Song of Praise (Psalm 22)

         22:1David sang this song to the LORD when he delivered him from his enemies and from Saul:

            2 “The LORD is my rock, my fortress, my deliverer,

                  3 my God, my refuge, my shield,

item.title: 1 Chronicles 23
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 23

Duties of Levites

1 When David got old, he appointed Solomon king over Israel. 2 He gathered the leaders with the priests and Levites. 3 The Levites 30 years old and up numbered 38,000. 4 Of these, 24,000 oversaw the work, 6,000 were officers and judges, 5 4,000 were gatekeepers, and 4,000 praised the LORD with instruments David made for that purpose. 6 He divided them according to the sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.     1 Ch 23:1-6

item.title: 1 Samuel 23
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 23

David Saves the Town of Keilah

1 People told David, “The Philistines are attacking Keilah and robbing the threshing floors.”

 2 David asked the LORD, “Should I attack these Philistines?”

“Yes, save Keilah.”

3 But David’s men said, “We’re afraid here in Judah. How much more at Keilah against the Philistines!”

item.title: 2 Chronicles 23
item.description:
2 Chronicles Chapter 23

Revolt Against Athaliah

1 In the seventh year, Jehoiada summoned the courage to make a pact with the commanders of hundreds: Azariah son of Joram/Jeroham, Ishmael son of Johanan, Azariah son of Obed, Maaseiah son of Adaiah, and Elishaphat son of Zichri. 2 They gathered to Jerusalem the Levites from the towns in Judah and the heads of families. 3 When the assembly made a pact with the king in God’s Temple, Jehoiada told them,

item.title: 2 Kings 23
item.description:
2 Kings Chapter 23

They took word back to the king.

2 Kg 22:8-20

Josiah Renews the Covenant

1 Then the king gathered the elders of Judah and Jerusalem. 2 He and the men of Judah, the residents in Jerusalem, the priests and prophets—people least and greatest—went up to the Temple. He read to them the covenant they’d found. 3 He stood by the pillar and made a covenant with the LORD to keep his commandments, testimonies, and statutes with all his heart and soul—to carry out the covenant written in that scroll. The people entered into the covenant.    2 Kg 23:1-3

item.title: 2 Samuel 23
item.description:
2 Samuel Chapter 23

1 David’s last words   

            “The oracle of David son of Jesse,

      the oracle of the man God honored,

                  the one anointed by Jacob’s God,

                  the sweet psalmist of Israel.

            2 “The Spirit of the LORD speaks by me;

item.title: 1 Chronicles 24
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 24

Duties of Priests

1 These were the divisions of Aaron’s descendants.
